qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Guest socket WebAPI test event permits arbitrary server-side requests and response disclosure
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory is project-import RCE caused by unsandboxed user-supplied scripts in imported project data. This finding instead targets the guest Socket.IO `device-webapi-request` SSRF path in `server/runtime/index.js` and `server/runtime/devices/httprequest/index.js`, with no imported project or script-execution root cause.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unauthenticated upload destination traversal writes attacker-controlled files into the web root
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory describes project-import RCE via malicious script content inside imported projects. This finding is a distinct `/api/upload` destination-traversal file-write bug; although it touches `server/api/projects/index.js`, the vulnerable branch is the upload path-building logic rather than project import or imported script execution.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described project-import RCE path. The session's qualifying findings are a guest Socket.IO SSRF and an unauthenticated upload destination traversal, both separate from the imported-script execution root cause described by CVE-2025-69983.
evidence: The advisory JSON and CVE record describe RCE via project import because imported project files can contain user-supplied scripts that are not sanitized or sandboxed before later execution.

In FUXA v1.2.7, `server/api/projects/index.js` exposes `POST /api/project` for project import/update, while `server/runtime/scripts/msm.js` compiles project script bodies from `script.code` with `Module._compile` and exposes privileged system functions through `global[...]`; that is the advisory-shaped import/script execution mechanism.

The `guest-socket-webapi-ssrf` finding instead reaches `socket.on(DEVICE_WEBAPI_REQUEST)` in `server/runtime/index.js`, calls `devices.getRequestResult`, performs `axios.get(property.address)`, and emits the fetched body back to clients. That is an SSRF root cause, not imported-script execution.

The `unauth-upload-destination-traversal` finding instead reaches `POST /api/upload` in `server/api/projects/index.js`, resolves attacker-controlled `destination` into a filesystem path, and writes with `fs.writeFileSync`. Sharing the same file as the advisory reference does not make it the same vulnerable endpoint or root cause.
